heading: 9.7.16.8.2.Packing and Unpacking
content:
Optionally, the following pack and unpack operations can be performed during the load and store:

1. Packing: two 16-bit chunks can be packed into a single 32-bit chunk in the register in `tcgen05.ld`
2. Unpacking: a single 32-bit chunk in the register can be unpacked into two 16-bit chunks in `tcgen05.st`

as shown in the [Figure 193](#tcgen05-ld-st-pack-unpack).



[Figure: tcgen05-ld-st-pack-unpack]
OVERVIEW OF THE FIGURE

This figure illustrates the Pack and Unpack operations for the tcgen05 load (ld) and store (st) instructions in NVIDIA PTX ISA. Specifically, it shows how 16-bit data elements are packed or unpacked when moving between a wide (packed) register layout and a narrow (unpacked) register layout across 32 threads (T0 through T31), each holding register values r0 and r1.

There are two named operations shown:
- tcgen05.st.unpack::16b — shown by arrows going from the LEFT (wide/packed) layout to the RIGHT (narrow/unpacked) layouts. Direction: left-to-right (top arrows).
- tcgen05.ld.pack::16b — shown by arrows going from the RIGHT (narrow/unpacked) layouts back to the LEFT (wide/packed) layout. Direction: right-to-left (bottom arrows).

---

LAYOUT DESCRIPTIONS

LEFT SIDE — PACKED LAYOUT (Wide, 32-bit per thread register)

This is a single tall column grid with 4 columns and 16 rows (covering threads T0–T31, registers r0 and r1).

The grid is 4 columns wide, labeled with a "32-bits" bracket spanning all 4 columns at the top.

Each cell contains a label of the form "Tx : ry" where x is the thread index (0–31) and y is the register index (0 or 1).

The 4 columns represent 4 separate 32-bit register slots (or sub-columns), each 8 bits wide (4 x 8 = 32 bits total per row).

Row-by-row contents (top to bottom), columns left to right:
Row 1:  T0:r0  | T1:r0  | T2:r0  | T3:r0
Row 2:  T4:r0  | T5:r0  | T6:r0  | T7:r0
Row 3:  T8:r0  | T9:r0  | T10:r0 | T11:r0
Row 4:  T12:r0 | T13:r0 | T14:r0 | T15:r0
Row 5:  T16:r0 | T17:r0 | T18:r0 | T19:r0
Row 6:  T20:r0 | T21:r0 | T22:r0 | T23:r0
Row 7:  T24:r0 | T25:r0 | T26:r0 | T27:r0
Row 8:  T28:r0 | T29:r0 | T30:r0 | T31:r0
Row 9:  T0:r1  | T1:r1  | T2:r1  | T3:r1
Row 10: T4:r1  | T5:r1  | T6:r1  | T7:r1
Row 11: T8:r1  | T9:r1  | T10:r1 | T11:r1
Row 12: T12:r1 | T13:r1 | T14:r1 | T15:r1
Row 13: T16:r1 | T17:r1 | T18:r1 | T19:r1
Row 14: T20:r1 | T21:r1 | T22:r1 | T23:r1
Row 15: T24:r1 | T25:r1 | T26:r1 | T27:r1
Row 16: T28:r1 | T29:r1 | T30:r1 | T31:r1

Color coding in the packed layout: Each thread's data is color-coded. The colors cycle through a set (green, yellow-green, teal/cyan, orange, gold, dark gray, blue, red-orange, etc.) to visually distinguish which thread's data maps to which unpacked column on the right side.

---

RIGHT SIDE — UNPACKED LAYOUT (Narrow, 16-bit per thread register)

There are 8 narrow single-column grids on the right side, arranged in pairs. Each narrow column contains 16 rows (T0–T31 for r0 and r1, but only half the threads per column compared to the packed layout).

Each narrow column is labeled "16-bits" at the bottom (two of the leftmost narrow columns are explicitly labeled "16-bits" each, with a bracket showing that two 16-bit columns together form "32-bits", and two 32-bit pairs form "64-bits").

The 8 narrow columns are grouped as follows (left to right on the right side):

GROUP 1 (leftmost pair, under "32-bits" bracket, under "64-bits" bracket):
Column A (16-bits):
T0:r0, T4:r0, T8:r0, T12:r0, T16:r0, T20:r0, T24:r0, T28:r0,
T0:r1, T4:r1, T8:r1, T12:r1, T16:r1, T20:r1, T24:r1, T28:r1

Column B (16-bits):
T0:r0, T4:r0, T8:r0, T12:r0, T16:r0, T20:r0, T24:r0, T28:r0,
T0:r1, T4:r1, T8:r1, T12:r1, T16:r1, T20:r1, T24:r1, T28:r1

(Columns A and B together = 32-bits; these correspond to threads T0, T4, T8, T12, T16, T20, T24, T28 — i.e., every 4th thread starting from T0)

GROUP 2 (second pair, also under "64-bits" bracket):
Column C (16-bits): T1:r0, T5:r0, T9:r0, T13:r0, T17:r0, T21:r0, T25:r0, T29:r0, T1:r1, T5:r1, T9:r1, T13:r1, T17:r1, T21:r1, T25:r1, T29:r1
Column D (16-bits): T1:r0, T5:r0, T9:r0, T13:r0, T17:r0, T21:r0, T25:r0, T29:r0, T1:r1, T5:r1, T9:r1, T13:r1, T17:r1, T21:r1, T25:r1, T29:r1

(Threads T1, T5, T9, T13, T17, T21, T25, T29)

GROUP 3 (third pair):
Column E: T2:r0, T6:r0, T10:r0, T14:r0, T18:r0, T22:r0, T26:r0, T30:r0, T2:r1, T6:r1, T10:r1, T14:r1, T18:r1, T22:r1, T26:r1, T30:r1
Column F: T2:r0, T6:r0, T10:r0, T14:r0, T18:r0, T22:r0, T26:r0, T30:r0, T2:r1, T6:r1, T10:r1, T14:r1, T18:r1, T22:r1, T26:r1, T30:r1

(Threads T2, T6, T10, T14, T18, T22, T26, T30)

GROUP 4 (rightmost pair):
Column G: T3:r0, T7:r0, T11:r0, T15:r0, T19:r0, T23:r0, T27:r0, T31:r0, T3:r1, T7:r1, T11:r1, T15:r1, T19:r1, T23:r1, T27:r1, T31:r1
Column H: T3:r0, T7:r0, T11:r0, T15:r0, T19:r0, T23:r0, T27:r0, T31:r0, T3:r1, T7:r1, T11:r1, T15:r1, T19:r1, T23:r1, T27:r1, T31:r1

(Threads T3, T7, T11, T15, T19, T23, T27, T31)

---

BIT-WIDTH ANNOTATIONS

- Each narrow (unpacked) column = 16-bits (labeled at the bottom of the two leftmost narrow columns)
- Two narrow columns together = 32-bits (bracket labeled "32-bits" spanning columns A+B)
- Four narrow columns together (two pairs) = 64-bits (bracket labeled "64-bits" spanning columns A+B+C+D)
- The packed left layout has a "32-bits" bracket spanning all 4 sub-columns

---

ARROWS AND DATA FLOW

TOP ARROWS (tcgen05.st.unpack::16b):
- A wide blue arrow at the top goes from the packed left layout rightward to the 8 unpacked narrow columns on the right.
- The label "tcgen05.st.unpack::16b" is written above this arrow.
- This indicates: when storing with unpack::16b, the 32-bit packed register data from each thread is split (unpacked) into two 16-bit values, distributed across two separate narrow columns.

BOTTOM ARROWS (tcgen05.ld.pack::16b):
- Blue arrows at the bottom go from the narrow unpacked columns (specifically the two leftmost narrow columns, columns A and B) back to the packed left layout.
- The label "tcgen05.ld.pack::16b" is written below these arrows.
- This indicates: when loading with pack::16b, two 16-bit values from two separate narrow columns are packed


Figure 193 Pack/Unpack operations for tcgen05 ld/st
